channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: French lawmakers approve a sweeping social media ban for children under 15
item.description: The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.
item.content:encoded:
The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.

(Image credit: Nicolas Garriga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Former Canadian finance minister on Trump's tariff plan and its impact
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Chrystia Freeland, former Canadian Finance Minister, about the potential impact of President Trump's proposed tariffs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Chrystia Freeland, former Canadian Finance Minister, about the potential impact of President Trump's proposed tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Aslam

item.title: Nicaragua's President Ortega says no more elections, extending his 20-year rule
item.description: The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.
item.content:encoded:
The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.

(Image credit: Alfredo Zuniga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
item.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.
item.content:encoded:
The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

(Image credit: Shekhar Yadav)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
item.description: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.
item.content:encoded:
Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

(Image credit: Mohammed Huwais)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthis declare a blockade of another key strait: Bab el-Mandeb
item.description: Commercial vessels have already begun rerouting in the Red Sea in response to the Iran-backed Houthis' blockade, according to ship trackers.
item.content:encoded:
Commercial vessels have already begun rerouting in the Red Sea in response to the Iran-backed Houthis' blockade, according to ship trackers.

(Image credit: Mohammed Huwais)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
item.description: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.
item.content:encoded:
The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

(Image credit: Bernat Armangue)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Powerball ticket sales begin Tuesday in the United Kingdom
item.description: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.
item.content:encoded:
Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
item.description: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen
